entries.statement: {r : ℕ} → Finset (Fin r) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CADSpace r → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CADSpace r → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Two assignments agree on the coordinates retained after witness elimination. -/
def SameOnEffectiveCoordinates {r : ℕ} (keep : Finset (Fin r))
    (x y : CADSpace r) : Prop :=
  ∀ i ∈ keep, x i = y i
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SameOnEffectiveCoordinates
entries.doc: Two assignments agree on the coordinates retained after witness elimination.

entries.statement: {n : ℕ} → (Fin n → ℝ × ℝ) → (Fin n → ℝ × ℝ) → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Equality of unordered supports in projective space, allowing an independent
nonzero rescaling of every displayed affine representative. -/
def SameProjectiveSupport {n : ℕ}
    (support support' : Fin n → ℝ × ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ z : ℝ × ℝ,
    (∃ i, ProjectivelyEquivalent z (support i)) ↔
      (∃ i, ProjectivelyEquivalent z (support' i))
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SameProjectiveSupport
entries.doc:
Equality of unordered supports in projective space, allowing an independent
nonzero rescaling of every displayed affine representative.

entries.statement: {m : ℕ} → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℝ) → MvPolynomial (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- A polynomial has the same sign at every pair of observable cumulant vectors in the cell.

The polynomial is first evaluated using the atlas map associated with each cumulant vector. -/
def SignInvariantOn {m : ℕ} (cell : Set (CumVec ℝ))
    (P : MvPolynomial (AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ t ∈ cell, ∀ t' ∈ cell,
    polynomialSign (MvPolynomial.eval (atlasObservableEval t) P) =
      polynomialSign (MvPolynomial.eval (atlasObservableEval t') P)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SignInvariantOn
entries.doc:
A polynomial has the same sign at every pair of observable cumulant vectors in the cell.

The polynomial is first evaluated using the atlas map associated with each cumulant vector.

entries.statement: {Ω : Type u_1} → [inst : MeasurableSpace Ω] → MeasureTheory.Measure Ω → {m : ℕ} → (Fin (m + 2) → Ω → ℝ) → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Every source law is non-Gaussian. -/
-- @node: ass:source-nongaussianity
def SourceNonGaussian (μ : Measure Ω) {m : ℕ} (S : Fin (m + 2) → Ω → ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ j, ¬ IsGaussianLaw (μ.map (S j))
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SourceNonGaussian
entries.doc: Every source law is non-Gaussian.

entries.statement: Prop
entries.source:
/-- The standard finite block-elimination order is effectively presentable for
every concrete retained coordinate block.  This is an existence statement for
the cited construction's canonical block order, not the false assertion that
every abstract `MonomialOrder` is effectively presentable. -/
def StandardFiniteBlockEliminationOrderInterface : Prop :=
  ∀ (r : ℕ) (keep : Finset (Fin r)),
    ∃ monomialOrder : MonomialOrder.{0, 0} (Fin r),
      ∃ suppliedOrder : EffectiveMonomialOrderCode r,
        suppliedOrder.Realizes monomialOrder ∧
          IsEffectiveEliminationMonomialOrder monomialOrder keep
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.StandardFiniteBlockEliminationOrderInterface
entries.doc:
The standard finite block-elimination order is effectively presentable for
every concrete retained coordinate block.  This is an existence statement for
the cited construction's canonical block order, not the false assertion that
every abstract `MonomialOrder` is effectively presentable.

entries.statement: ℕ → ℕ → Type
entries.source:
/-- **Structural-model witness** `M`: a bivariate LvLiNGAM structural model
carrying its observational law `P_M = law`, its directed edge `D(M) = edge`, and a
direction-specific real moment-feasible parameter list `param ∈ F^b_{m,K}` (with
`b = edge`) that realizes the truncated cumulant observation `T_K(P_M)` through
order `K`, together with the corresponding LvLiNGAM class membership.  This carries
the model / representation identity (`P_M` and its feasible parameters) that a bare
observational measure would lose.
@realizes M,P_M,D(M),M^{sep}_{m,K}(model `M`: law `P_M`, edge `D(M)`, feasible param) -/
structure StructuralModel (m K : ℕ) where
  /-- The observational law `P_M ∈ Laws(ℝ²)`. -/
  law : Measure (ℝ × ℝ)
  /-- The directed edge `D(M)`. -/
  edge : Direction
  /-- The model's own direction-specific real moment-feasible representation parameter. -/
  param : ParamSpace ℝ m
  /-- Real feasibility of `param` (nonzero edge, distinct slopes, source realizability). -/
  feasible : param ∈ realFeasibleRegion m K
  /-- `M` is a forward (resp. reverse) LvLiNGAM model represented by its own
  feasible parameter: the parameter's slopes and source-cumulant weights are
  exactly those of the sources generating `P_M`, and its simultaneous binary-form
  image is the truncated cumulant of `P_M`. -/
  realizes :
    (edge = Direction.forward →
        ForwardLvLiNGAMRep law m K param ∧
        forwardCumulantMap m K param = truncatedCumulant law Prod.fst Prod.snd K) ∧
    (edge = Direction.reverse →
        ReverseLvLiNGAMRep law m K param ∧
        reverseCumulantMap m K param = truncatedCumulant law Prod.fst Prod.snd K)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.StructuralModel
entries.doc:
**Structural-model witness** `M`: a bivariate LvLiNGAM structural model
carrying its observational law `P_M = law`, its directed edge `D(M) = edge`, and a
direction-specific real moment-feasible parameter list `param ∈ F^b_{m,K}` (with
`b = edge`) that realizes the truncated cumulant observation `T_K(P_M)` through
order `K`, together with the corresponding LvLiNGAM class membership.  This carries
the model / representation identity (`P_M` and its feasible parameters) that a bare
observational measure would lose.
@realizes M,P_M,D(M),M^{sep}_{m,K}(model `M`: law `P_M`, edge `D(M)`, feasible param)

entries.statement: (m : ℕ) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℝ → (Fin (m + 2) → ℝ × ℝ) → Prop
entries.source:
/-- The retained divided-power cumulant blocks of `t` admit the displayed
`m+2`-direction support with order-specific source weights. -/
def SupportExplainsCumulantBlocks (m : ℕ) (t : CumVec ℝ)
    (support : Fin (m + 2) → ℝ × ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ weights : Fin (m + 2) → ℕ → ℝ,
    ∀ r a, 2 ≤ r → r ≤ 2 * m + 2 → a ≤ r →
      t r a = ∑ j, weights j r * (support j).1 ^ (r - a) * (support j).2 ^ a
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SupportExplainsCumulantBlocks
entries.doc:
The retained divided-power cumulant blocks of `t` admit the displayed
`m+2`-direction support with order-specific source weights.

entries.statement: Prop
entries.source:
/-- **Tarski–Seidenberg projection theorem** — BCR Thm 2.2.1; BPR Ch. 2 (Projection Theorem for
Semi-Algebraic Sets).

*Statement of record.*  The image of a semialgebraic subset of `ℝ^{r+1}` under the projection that
forgets the last coordinate is a semialgebraic subset of `ℝ^r`.  Equivalently: semialgebraic
conditions are closed under existential quantification, so a quantified polynomial condition is
equivalent to a quantifier-free one.

This is the *set-theoretic* projection/quantifier-elimination statement, not the effective decision
procedure.  Universally quantified over the ambient dimension and the set; ASSUMED, not proved. -/
def TarskiSeidenbergProjection : Prop :=
  ∀ (r : ℕ) (S : Set (CADSpace (r + 1))),
    IsSemialgebraicSet S →
      IsSemialgebraicSet { x : CADSpace r | ∃ z : ℝ, (Fin.snoc x z : CADSpace (r + 1)) ∈ S }
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.TarskiSeidenbergProjection
entries.doc:
**Tarski–Seidenberg projection theorem** — BCR Thm 2.2.1; BPR Ch. 2 (Projection Theorem for
Semi-Algebraic Sets).

*Statement of record.*  The image of a semialgebraic subset of `ℝ^{r+1}` under the projection that
forgets the last coordinate is a semialgebraic subset of `ℝ^r`.  Equivalently: semialgebraic
conditions are closed under existential quantification, so a quantified polynomial condition is
equivalent to a quantifier-free one.

This is the *set-theoretic* projection/quantifier-elimination statement, not the effective decision
procedure.  Universally quantified over the ambient dimension and the set; ASSUMED, not proved.

entries.statement: ℕ → ℕ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Universal source-matched combined complexity certificate. The constants
and all three uniform machine codes are fixed before every rational or Gaussian-
rational algebra batch and before the rational CAD/QE input. In the dependent
clause, the caller fixes the cross-source shared-coordinate relation before the
two elimination results and their exact intersection result are returned; only
then is a CAD/QE job consuming that pipeline's intersection basis quantified.
The one displayed inequality charges the same returned pipeline and CAD/QE
result together; partial-recursive fuel is deliberately absent from this cost
statement. -/
def UniversalEffectiveCombinedGroebnerCADBound (a b : ℕ) : Prop :=
  1 ≤ a ∧
    ∃ rationalAlgebraMachine gaussianAlgebraMachine rationalCADMachine :
        Nat.Partrec.Code,
      (∀ (rationalJobs : List (EffectiveGroebnerJobOver ℚ))
          (gaussianJobs : List (EffectiveGroebnerJobOver GaussianRational))
          (cadJob : EffectiveRationalCADJob) (N s D : ℕ),
        1 ≤ D →
        effectiveGroebnerBatchSourcePolynomialCount rationalJobs +
              effectiveGroebnerBatchSourcePolynomialCount gaussianJobs +
              cadJob.sourcePolynomialCount ≤ s →
        EffectiveGroebnerBatchInputsBounded rationalJobs N D →
        EffectiveGroebnerBatchInputsBounded gaussianJobs N D →
        cadJob.r ≤ N → cadJob.DegreeBoundedBy D →
          ∃ rationalResults : EffectiveGroebnerBatchResultsOver ℚ
                rationalAlgebraMachine rationalJobs,
            ∃ gaussianResults : EffectiveGroebnerBatchResultsOver GaussianRational
                gaussianAlgebraMachine gaussianJobs,
              ∃ cadResult : EffectiveRationalCADCompletedJob rationalCADMachine cadJob,
                rationalResults.symbolicOperationCount +
                    gaussianResults.symbolicOperationCount +
                    cadResult.result.payload.symbolicOperationCount ≤
                  (max 2 (s * D)) ^ (2 ^ (a * N + b))) ∧
      (∀ (forwardJob reverseJob : EffectiveGroebnerJobOver ℚ)
          (sharedCoordinateRelation : Fin forwardJob.r → Fin reverseJob.r → Prop),
        IsJointlyPresentableSharedCoordinateRelation sharedCoordinateRelation →
          ∃ pipeline : EffectiveDependentRationalEliminationPipeline
              rationalAlgebraMachine forwardJob reverseJob sharedCoordinateRelation,
          ∀ (dependentCADJob : EffectiveDependentRationalCADJob pipeline)
              (N s D : ℕ),
            1 ≤ D →
            forwardJob.sourcePolynomialCount + reverseJob.sourcePolynomialCount +
                  dependentCADJob.job.sourcePolynomialCount ≤ s →
            forwardJob.r ≤ N → reverseJob.r ≤ N →
            dependentCADJob.job.r ≤ N →
            pipeline.DegreeBoundedBy D →
            dependentCADJob.job.DegreeBoundedBy D →
              ∃ cadResult : EffectiveRationalCADCompletedJob rationalCADMachine
                    dependentCADJob.job,
                pipeline.symbolicOperationCount +
                    cadResult.result.payload.symbolicOperationCount ≤
                  (max 2 (s * D)) ^ (2 ^ (a * N + b)))
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.UniversalEffectiveCombinedGroebnerCADBound
entries.doc:
Universal source-matched combined complexity certificate. The constants
and all three uniform machine codes are fixed before every rational or Gaussian-
rational algebra batch and before the rational CAD/QE input. In the dependent
clause, the caller fixes the cross-source shared-coordinate relation before the
two elimination results and their exact intersection result are returned; only
then is a CAD/QE job consuming that pipeline's intersection basis quantified.
The one displayed inequality charges the same returned pipeline and CAD/QE
result together; partial-recursive fuel is deliberately absent from this cost
statement.

entries.statement: ℕ → ℕ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Backward-compatible public name used by atlas complexity certificates. It
now denotes the single combined algebra-plus-CAD batch bound, not a separate
rational-CAD-only inequality. -/
def UniversalEffectiveRationalGroebnerCADBound (a b : ℕ) : Prop :=
  UniversalEffectiveCombinedGroebnerCADBound a b
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.UniversalEffectiveRationalGroebnerCADBound
entries.doc:
Backward-compatible public name used by atlas complexity certificates. It
now denotes the single combined algebra-plus-CAD batch bound, not a separate
rational-CAD-only inequality.

entries.statement: {r : ℕ} → Finset (Fin r) → MvPolynomial (Fin r) ℚ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- A polynomial uses only the retained variables of an elimination block. -/
def UsesOnlyEffectiveVariables {r : ℕ} (keep : Finset (Fin r))
    (P : MvPolynomial (Fin r) ℚ) : Prop :=
  P.vars ⊆ keep
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.UsesOnlyEffectiveVariables
entries.doc: A polynomial uses only the retained variables of an elimination block.

entries.statement: {K : Type} → [inst : Field K] → {r : ℕ} → Finset (Fin r) → MvPolynomial (Fin r) K → Prop
entries.source:
/-- A polynomial over `K` uses only the retained variables of an elimination block. -/
def UsesOnlyEffectiveVariablesOver {K : Type} [Field K] {r : ℕ}
    (keep : Finset (Fin r)) (P : MvPolynomial (Fin r) K) : Prop :=
  P.vars ⊆ keep
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.UsesOnlyEffectiveVariablesOver
entries.doc: A polynomial over `K` uses only the retained variables of an elimination block.

entries.statement: ℕ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Space of the structural-complexity index `m ∈ {1, 2, …}`: at least one latent
confounder.  Every statement in this development is stated for `m` with this
well-formedness clause.
@realizes m(m ≥ 1, i.e. m ∈ {1,2,…}) -/
def ValidComplexity (m : ℕ) : Prop := 1 ≤ m
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ValidComplexity
entries.doc:
Space of the structural-complexity index `m ∈ {1, 2, …}`: at least one latent
confounder.  Every statement in this development is stated for `m` with this
well-formedness clause.
@realizes m(m ≥ 1, i.e. m ∈ {1,2,…})

entries.statement: ℕ → ℕ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Space of the truncation endpoint `L ∈ {2, …, K}` with `K = 2m + 2`: the order
variable ranges over the retained cumulant orders.
@realizes L(2 ≤ L ≤ 2m + 2) -/
def ValidOrder (m L : ℕ) : Prop := 2 ≤ L ∧ L ≤ 2 * m + 2
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ValidOrder

entries.statement: ∀ {m : ℕ} (γ : ℂ) (ρ : Fin m → ℂ), MvPolynomial.X 0 ∣ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.supportAnnihilator (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardLoading m γ ρ)
entries.source:
/-- For the forward loading family, the index-`(m+1)` factor equals `X 0`, so `X 0 ∣ Q_D`. -/
lemma X0_dvd_supportAnnihilator_forward {m : ℕ} (γ : ℂ) (ρ : Fin m → ℂ) :
    (MvPolynomial.X (0 : Fin 2)) ∣ supportAnnihilator (forwardLoading m γ ρ) := by
  rw [supportAnnihilator]
  refine dvd_trans ?_ (Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem _
    (Finset.mem_univ (⟨m + 1, by omega⟩ : Fin (m + 2))))
  simp [forwardLoading]
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.X0_dvd_supportAnnihilator_forward
entries.doc: For the forward loading family, the index-`(m+1)` factor equals `X 0`, so `X 0 ∣ Q_D`.

entries.statement: ∀ {m : ℕ} (δ : ℂ) (σ : Fin m → ℂ), MvPolynomial.X 1 ∣ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.supportAnnihilator (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.reverseLoading m δ σ)
entries.source:
/-- Mirror fixed-axis divisibility for the reverse loading family. -/
lemma X1_dvd_supportAnnihilator_reverse {m : ℕ} (δ : ℂ) (σ : Fin m → ℂ) :
    (MvPolynomial.X (1 : Fin 2)) ∣ supportAnnihilator (reverseLoading m δ σ) := by
  rw [supportAnnihilator]
  refine dvd_trans ?_ (Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem _
    (Finset.mem_univ (⟨0, by omega⟩ : Fin (m + 2))))
  simp [reverseLoading]
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.X1_dvd_supportAnnihilator_reverse
entries.doc: Mirror fixed-axis divisibility for the reverse loading family.

entries.statement: {m : ℕ} → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace ℂ m → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace ℂ m)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleOrbit
entries.doc:
Orbit of `θ` under the admissible source-swap `G_m` action (the admissible
relabelings of the middle latent block).

entries.statement: {R : Type u_1} → (m : ℕ) → Equiv.Perm (Fin m) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m
entries.source:
/-- Admissible source swap: `π ∈ G_m = Equiv.Perm (Fin m)` acts by relabeling the
latent slopes `ρ_i` (resp. `σ_i`) and their weights `c_{ir}` (resp. `d_{ir}`)
simultaneously, while fixing indices `0` and `m + 1`.  The same map realizes both
arrow actions.
@realizes G_m,pi,b(π-relabeling of the middle source block) -/
-- @node: def:admissible-source-swaps
def admissibleSourceSwap {R : Type*} (m : ℕ) (π : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
    (θ : ParamSpace R m) : ParamSpace R m :=
  (θ.1, fun i => θ.2.1 (π i), fun j r => θ.2.2 (permMiddle m π j) r)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap
entries.doc:
Admissible source swap: `π ∈ G_m = Equiv.Perm (Fin m)` acts by relabeling the
latent slopes `ρ_i` (resp. `σ_i`) and their weights `c_{ir}` (resp. `d_{ir}`)
simultaneously, while fixing indices `0` and `m + 1`.  The same map realizes both
arrow actions.
@realizes G_m,pi,b(π-relabeling of the middle source block)

entries.statement: {R : Type u_1} → (m : ℕ) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow → Equiv.Perm (Fin m) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m
entries.source:
/-- Arrow-tagged admissible source swap: the `G_m` action on arrow `b`.  The same
middle-block relabeling formula realizes both the forward (`right`) action on
`(ρ_i, c_{ir})` and the reverse (`left`) action on `(σ_i, d_{ir})`, so `b` is a
tag; indices `0` and `m + 1` are fixed in both cases.
@realizes G_m,pi,b(π-relabeling tagged by arrow b; same formula for both) -/
def admissibleSourceSwapArrow {R : Type*} (m : ℕ) (_b : Arrow) (π : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
    (θ : ParamSpace R m) : ParamSpace R m :=
  admissibleSourceSwap m π θ
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwapArrow
entries.doc:
Arrow-tagged admissible source swap: the `G_m` action on arrow `b`.  The same
middle-block relabeling formula realizes both the forward (`right`) action on
`(ρ_i, c_{ir})` and the reverse (`left`) action on `(σ_i, d_{ir})`, so `b` is a
tag; indices `0` and `m + 1` are fixed in both cases.
@realizes G_m,pi,b(π-relabeling tagged by arrow b; same formula for both)

entries.statement: {R : Type u_1} → (m : ℕ) → Equiv.Perm (Fin m) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow × CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow × CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m
entries.source:
/-- The `G_m` action on the tagged space `Arrow × ParamSpace`: relabel the middle
source block and **preserve the arrow tag** `b`.  The swap fixes indices `0`, `m + 1`,
so it never converts a forward (`right`) axis pattern into a reverse (`left`) one; the
tag component is carried unchanged.
@realizes G_m,pi,b(tag-preserving G_m action on Arrow × ParamSpace) -/
def admissibleSourceSwapTagged {R : Type*} (m : ℕ) (π : Equiv.Perm (Fin m)) :
    Arrow × ParamSpace R m → Arrow × ParamSpace R m :=
  fun p => (p.1, admissibleSourceSwap m π p.2)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwapTagged
entries.doc:

so it never converts a forward (`right`) axis pattern into a reverse (`left`) one; the

@realizes G_m,pi,b(tag-preserving G_m action on Arrow × ParamSpace)

entries.statement:
∀ (m L : ℕ),
  CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ValidComplexity m →
    CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ValidOrder m L →
      ∀ (π : Equiv.Perm (Fin m)) (θc ηc : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace ℂ m) (θ η : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace ℝ m) (Pforward Preverse : MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ)),
        (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardCumulantMap m L (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θc) = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardCumulantMap m L θc ∧ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.reverseCumulantMap m L (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π ηc) = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.reverseCumulantMap m L ηc) ∧
          (θ ∈ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.realFeasibleRegion m L → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ ∈ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.realFeasibleRegion m L) ∧
            (η ∈ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.realFeasibleRegion m L → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η ∈ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.realFeasibleRegion m L) ∧
              (∀ {Ω : Type} [inst : MeasurableSpace Ω] (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω) (S : Fin (m + 2) → Ω → ℝ) (X Y : Ω → ℝ),
                  θ ∈ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.realFeasibleRegion m L →
                    MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ →
                      Pforward = MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun ω => (X ω, Y ω)) μ →
                        CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.IndependentSources μ S →
                          (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), ∫ (ω : Ω), S j ω ∂μ = 0) →
                            CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SourceNonGaussian μ S →
                              CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.FiniteCumulants μ S (2 * m + 2) →
                                CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardAxisModel X Y S θ.1 θ.2.1 →
                                  CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardNonCollinear θ.1 θ.2.1 →
                                    CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardNonzeroEdge θ.1 →
                                      (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)) (r : ℕ), 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → Causalean.Stat.MomentProblems.sourceCumulant μ (S j) r = θ.2.2 j r) →
                                        (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.IndependentSources μ fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) ∧
                                          (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), ∫ (ω : Ω), S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π j) ω ∂μ = 0) ∧
                                            (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SourceNonGaussian μ fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) ∧
                                              CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.FiniteCumulants μ (fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) (2 * m + 2) ∧
                                                CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardAxisModel X Y (fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).1 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).2.1 ∧
                                                  CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardNonCollinear (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).1 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).2.1 ∧ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardNonzeroEdge (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).1 ∧ (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)) (r : ℕ), 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → Causalean.Stat.MomentProblems.sourceCumulant μ (S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π j)) r = (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).2.2 j r) ∧ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ForwardLvLiNGAM Pforward m) ∧
                (∀ {Ω : Type} [inst : MeasurableSpace Ω] (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω) (S : Fin (m + 2) → Ω → ℝ) (X Y : Ω → ℝ),
                    η ∈ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.realFeasibleRegion m L →
                      MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ →
                        Preverse = MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun ω => (X ω, Y ω)) μ →
                          CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.IndependentSources μ S →
                            (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), ∫ (ω : Ω), S j ω ∂μ = 0) →
                              CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SourceNonGaussian μ S →
                                CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.FiniteCumulants μ S (2 * m + 2) →
                                  CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseAxisModel X Y S η.1 η.2.1 →
                                    CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseNonCollinear η.1 η.2.1 →
                                      CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseNonzeroEdge η.1 →
                                        (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)) (r : ℕ), 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → Causalean.Stat.MomentProblems.sourceCumulant μ (S j) r = η.2.2 j r) →
                                          (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.IndependentSources μ fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) ∧
                                            (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), ∫ (ω : Ω), S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π j) ω ∂μ = 0) ∧
                                              (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.SourceNonGaussian μ fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) ∧
                                                CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.FiniteCumulants μ (fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) (2 * m + 2) ∧
                                                  CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseAxisModel X Y (fun i => S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π i)) (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η).1 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η).2.1 ∧
                                                    CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseNonCollinear (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η).1 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η).2.1 ∧ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseNonzeroEdge (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η).1 ∧ (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)) (r : ℕ), 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → Causalean.Stat.MomentProblems.sourceCumulant μ (S (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π j)) r = (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η).2.2 j r) ∧ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ReverseLvLiNGAM Preverse m) ∧
                  (∀ (M : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.StructuralModel m L), M.edge = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Direction.forward → M.param = θ → ∃ M', M'.law = M.law ∧ M'.edge = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Direction.forward ∧ M'.param = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θ) ∧
                    (∀ (M : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.StructuralModel m L), M.edge = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Direction.reverse → M.param = η → ∃ M', M'.law = M.law ∧ M'.edge = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Direction.reverse ∧ M'.param = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π η) ∧
                      ((∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwapArrow m CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.right π θc).2.2 j = fun r => θc.2.2 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π j) r) ∧
                          (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwapArrow m CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.left π ηc).2.2 j = fun r => ηc.2.2 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.permMiddle m π j) r) ∧
                            (∀ (j : Fin (m + 2)), ↑j = 0 ∨ ↑j = m + 1 → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardLoading m (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θc).1 (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m π θc).2.1 j = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardLoading m θc.1 θc.2.1 j) ∧
                              (∀ (π' : Equiv.Perm (Fin m)), (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwapTagged m π' (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.right, θc)).1 = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.right ∧ (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwapTagged m π' (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.left, ηc)).1 = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.left) ∧ Disjoint (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.arrowTaggedOrbit m CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.right θc) (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.arrowTaggedOrbit m CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.left ηc)) ∧
                        ∀ (σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin m)), CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardCumulantMap m L (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m σ θc) = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.forwardCumulantMap m L θc ∧ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.reverseCumulantMap m L (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSourceSwap m σ ηc) = CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.reverseCumulantMap m L ηc
entries.source:
/-- **Admissible swaps preserve direction.**  For each `π ∈ G_m`:

* (map invariance) the forward and reverse cumulant maps are invariant under the
  admissible source swap `θ ↦ π · θ` — the Finset-sum reindexing crux;
* (real-feasibility preservation) the swap maps each real feasible region into
  itself (`θ ∈ F^b ⇒ π · θ ∈ F^b`);
* (full LvLiNGAM preservation, model tied to its weights) **for a real-feasible
  parameter** (`θ ∈ F^right_{m,L}`, resp. `η ∈ F^left_{m,L}`, within the valid order
  range `ValidOrder m L` — matching the paper's "if a forward structural model has
  real-feasible parameter `θ`" hypothesis), relabeling the latent
  sources by `π` (`S'_i = S_{π(i)}`, fixing `0` and `m + 1`) preserves independence,
  centering, non-Gaussianity, the finite moments, noncollinearity, the nonzero edge,
  the forward (resp. reverse) structural equation, and hence the observed pair
  `(X, Y)`, its observational law `P_M`, and the directed edge `D(M)`.  Each model's
  *actual* source cumulants are its parameter weights (`Cum(S_j) = θ.2.2 j` resp.
  `η.2.2 j`), and the swap transports this link to the relabeled model (its source
  cumulants are the swapped weights);
* (arrow-tag preservation and opposite-arrow orbit disjointness) the `G_m` action on
  `Arrow × ParamSpace` **preserves the arrow tag** `b` (the swap fixes indices `0` and
  `m + 1` and never converts a forward axis pattern into a reverse one), so the
  right-tagged and left-tagged `G_m`-orbits are **disjoint**; hence quotienting a
  same-arrow fiber by `G_m` identifies only source labels and never identifies
  opposite arrows. -/
-- @node: lem:admissible-swaps-preserve-direction
lemma admissibleSwaps_preserve_direction (m L : ℕ)
    (hm : ValidComplexity m) (hL : ValidOrder m L)
    (π : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
    -- COMPLEX parameters carry the map/orbit invariance: the note states invariance and
    -- quotient behaviour for the **complex** `Θ`-fibers (`fiberCorrespondence`,
    -- `quotientFiber`, `admissibleOrbit` are all complex), so specializing those clauses
    -- to `ℝ` would understate the claim.
    (θc ηc : ParamSpace ℂ m)
    -- REAL parameters carry the feasible structural-model preservation: real feasibility
    -- (`realFeasibleRegion`) and the LvLiNGAM source witnesses are real objects.
    (θ η : ParamSpace ℝ m)
    (Pforward Preverse : Measure (ℝ × ℝ)) :
    -- (1) map invariance (both arrows), on the COMPLEX fibers:
    (forwardCumulantMap m L (admissibleSourceSwap m π θc) = forwardCumulantMap m L θc ∧
     reverseCumulantMap m L (admissibleSourceSwap m π ηc) = reverseCumulantMap m L ηc) ∧
    -- (2) real-feasibility preservation (both arrows):
    (θ ∈ realFeasibleRegion m L → admissibleSourceSwap m π θ ∈ realFeasibleRegion m L) ∧
    (η ∈ realFeasibleRegion m L → admissibleSourceSwap m π η ∈ realFeasibleRegion m L) ∧
    -- (3) full forward LvLiNGAM preservation under the source relabeling
    -- `S'_i = S_{π(i)}` (independence, centering, non-Gaussianity, moments,
    -- noncollinearity, nonzero edge, structural equation, pushforward law):
    (∀ {Ω : Type} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (μ : Measure Ω) (S : Fin (m + 2) → Ω → ℝ)
        (X Y : Ω → ℝ),
        -- the paper conditions model preservation on the forward parameter being
        -- real-feasible (`θ ∈ F^right_{m,L}`) and on the declared valid order range:
        θ ∈ realFeasibleRegion m L → IsProbabilityMeasure μ →
        Pforward = μ.map (fun ω => (X ω, Y ω)) →
        IndependentSources μ S → (∀ j, ∫ ω, S j ω ∂μ = 0) → SourceNonGaussian μ S →
        FiniteCumulants μ S (2 * m + 2) → ForwardAxisModel X Y S θ.1 θ.2.1 →
        ForwardNonCollinear θ.1 θ.2.1 → ForwardNonzeroEdge θ.1 →
        -- the model's actual source cumulants ARE its parameter weights `θ.2.2`:
        (∀ j, ∀ r, 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → sourceCumulant μ (S j) r = θ.2.2 j r) →
          IndependentSources μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) ∧
          (∀ j, ∫ ω, S (permMiddle m π j) ω ∂μ = 0) ∧
          SourceNonGaussian μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) ∧
          FiniteCumulants μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) (2 * m + 2) ∧
          ForwardAxisModel X Y (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i))
            (admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).1 (admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).2.1 ∧
          ForwardNonCollinear (admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).1 (admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).2.1 ∧
          ForwardNonzeroEdge (admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).1 ∧
          -- the relabeled model's source cumulants are the SWAPPED weights, so the
          -- model–parameter link is transported by the swap:
          (∀ j, ∀ r, 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L →
              sourceCumulant μ (S (permMiddle m π j)) r = (admissibleSourceSwap m π θ).2.2 j r) ∧
          -- the relabeled witnesses therefore realize the same observational law
          -- inside the forward LvLiNGAM class, which is the formal direction verdict:
          ForwardLvLiNGAM Pforward m) ∧
    -- reverse mirror:
    (∀ {Ω : Type} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (μ : Measure Ω) (S : Fin (m + 2) → Ω → ℝ)
        (X Y : Ω → ℝ),
        -- reverse mirror, conditioned on the reverse parameter being real-feasible
        -- (`η ∈ F^left_{m,L}`) and on the declared valid order range:
        η ∈ realFeasibleRegion m L → IsProbabilityMeasure μ →
        Preverse = μ.map (fun ω => (X ω, Y ω)) →
        IndependentSources μ S → (∀ j, ∫ ω, S j ω ∂μ = 0) → SourceNonGaussian μ S →
        FiniteCumulants μ S (2 * m + 2) → ReverseAxisModel X Y S η.1 η.2.1 →
        ReverseNonCollinear η.1 η.2.1 → ReverseNonzeroEdge η.1 →
        -- the model's actual source cumulants ARE its parameter weights `η.2.2`:
        (∀ j, ∀ r, 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → sourceCumulant μ (S j) r = η.2.2 j r) →
          IndependentSources μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) ∧
          (∀ j, ∫ ω, S (permMiddle m π j) ω ∂μ = 0) ∧
          SourceNonGaussian μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) ∧
          FiniteCumulants μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) (2 * m + 2) ∧
          ReverseAxisModel X Y (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i))
            (admissibleSourceSwap m π η).1 (admissibleSourceSwap m π η).2.1 ∧
          ReverseNonCollinear (admissibleSourceSwap m π η).1 (admissibleSourceSwap m π η).2.1 ∧
          ReverseNonzeroEdge (admissibleSourceSwap m π η).1 ∧
          -- the relabeled model's source cumulants are the SWAPPED weights:
          (∀ j, ∀ r, 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L →
              sourceCumulant μ (S (permMiddle m π j)) r = (admissibleSourceSwap m π η).2.2 j r) ∧
          -- reverse mirror: the same observational law remains represented in the
          -- reverse LvLiNGAM class, hence its structural arrow stays `Y → X`:
          ReverseLvLiNGAM Preverse m) ∧
    -- (4) explicit structural-model direction verdicts.  These clauses state the
    -- paper's `D(M) = X → Y` / `D(M) = Y → X` conclusion on the actual model
    -- carrier, rather than leaving direction implicit in class membership.  The
    -- relabeled model has the same observational law and the swapped feasible
    -- parameter, while its `edge` field records the unchanged causal direction.
    (∀ M : StructuralModel m L,
        M.edge = Direction.forward → M.param = θ →
        ∃ M' : StructuralModel m L,
          M'.law = M.law ∧ M'.edge = Direction.forward ∧
          M'.param = admissibleSourceSwap m π θ) ∧
    (∀ M : StructuralModel m L,
        M.edge = Direction.reverse → M.param = η →
        ∃ M' : StructuralModel m L,
          M'.law = M.law ∧ M'.edge = Direction.reverse ∧
          M'.param = admissibleSourceSwap m π η) ∧
    -- (5) arrow-tagged quotient/orbit distinctness.  The admissible `G_m` swap
    -- relabels the source WEIGHT family, so each relabeled source's cumulants are
    -- exactly the original weights `θ.2.2` (resp. `η.2.2`) at the permuted index —
    -- linking the actual sources' cumulants to `θ.2.2`/`η.2.2`.  The boundary loading
    -- directions (indices `0`, `m+1`) are fixed by the swap.  And for EVERY pair of
    -- admissible swaps on the two arrows (`Arrow.right` for `θ`, `Arrow.left` for `η`)
    -- the forward fixed vertical axis differs from the reverse fixed horizontal axis:
    -- no admissible swap converts a forward axis pattern into a reverse one, so the
    -- two arrow-tagged `G_m`-orbits are never identified:
    ((∀ j : Fin (m + 2),
        (admissibleSourceSwapArrow m Arrow.right π θc).2.2 j
          = fun r => θc.2.2 (permMiddle m π j) r) ∧
     (∀ j : Fin (m + 2),
        (admissibleSourceSwapArrow m Arrow.left π ηc).2.2 j
          = fun r => ηc.2.2 (permMiddle m π j) r) ∧
     (∀ j : Fin (m + 2), j.val = 0 ∨ j.val = m + 1 →
        forwardLoading m (admissibleSourceSwap m π θc).1 (admissibleSourceSwap m π θc).2.1 j
          = forwardLoading m θc.1 θc.2.1 j) ∧
     -- arrow-tag preservation: the `G_m` action on `Arrow × ParamSpace` fixes the tag
     -- `b`, so every image of a right-tagged (resp. left-tagged) parameter stays
     -- right-tagged (resp. left-tagged):
     (∀ π' : Equiv.Perm (Fin m),
        (admissibleSourceSwapTagged m π' (Arrow.right, θc)).1 = Arrow.right ∧
        (admissibleSourceSwapTagged m π' (Arrow.left, ηc)).1 = Arrow.left) ∧
     -- hence the right-tagged and left-tagged `G_m`-orbits are DISJOINT: no admissible
     -- swap converts a forward-tagged parameter into a reverse-tagged one, so
     -- quotienting a same-arrow fiber by `G_m` never identifies opposite arrows:
     Disjoint (arrowTaggedOrbit m Arrow.right θc) (arrowTaggedOrbit m Arrow.left ηc)) ∧
    -- (6) same-arrow map invariance under every relabeling `σ`, on the COMPLEX fibers:
    (∀ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin m),
        forwardCumulantMap m L (admissibleSourceSwap m σ θc) = forwardCumulantMap m L θc ∧
        reverseCumulantMap m L (admissibleSourceSwap m σ ηc) = reverseCumulantMap m L ηc) := by
  refine ⟨⟨forwardCumulantMap_admissibleSourceSwap m L π θc,
      reverseCumulantMap_admissibleSourceSwap m L π ηc⟩,
    realFeasibleRegion_admissibleSourceSwap m L π θ,
    realFeasibleRegion_admissibleSourceSwap m L π η, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · intro Ω inst μ S X Y _hθ hprob hP hind hcenter hng hfinite haxis hnc hedge hcum
    have hind' : IndependentSources μ (fun i => S (permMiddle m π i)) :=
  -- … truncated; follow the source link for the rest …
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.admissibleSwaps_preserve_direction
entries.doc:
**Admissible swaps preserve direction.**  For each `π ∈ G_m`:

* (map invariance) the forward and reverse cumulant maps are invariant under the
  admissible source swap `θ ↦ π · θ` — the Finset-sum reindexing crux;
* (real-feasibility preservation) the swap maps each real feasible region into
  itself (`θ ∈ F^b ⇒ π · θ ∈ F^b`);
* (full LvLiNGAM preservation, model tied to its weights) **for a real-feasible
  parameter** (`θ ∈ F^right_{m,L}`, resp. `η ∈ F^left_{m,L}`, within the valid order
  range `ValidOrder m L` — matching the paper's "if a forward structural model has
  real-feasible parameter `θ`" hypothesis), relabeling the latent
  sources by `π` (`S'_i = S_{π(i)}`, fixing `0` and `m + 1`) preserves independence,
  centering, non-Gaussianity, the finite moments, noncollinearity, the nonzero edge,
  the forward (resp. reverse) structural equation, and hence the observed pair
  `(X, Y)`, its observational law `P_M`, and the directed edge `D(M)`.  Each model's
  *actual* source cumulants are its parameter weights (`Cum(S_j) = θ.2.2 j` resp.
  `η.2.2 j`), and the swap transports this link to the relabeled model (its source
  cumulants are the swapped weights);
* (arrow-tag preservation and opposite-arrow orbit disjointness) the `G_m` action on
  `Arrow × ParamSpace` **preserves the arrow tag** `b` (the swap fixes indices `0` and
  `m + 1` and never converts a forward axis pattern into a reverse one), so the
  right-tagged and left-tagged `G_m`-orbits are **disjoint**; hence quotienting a
  same-arrow fiber by `G_m` identifies only source labels and never identifies
  opposite arrows.

entries.statement: {R : Type u_1} → (m : ℕ) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow × CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.arrowTaggedOrbit
entries.doc:
Arrow-tagged `G_m`-orbit of `(b, θ)`: its images under all admissible swaps, every
member carrying the same tag `b`.  Right-tagged and left-tagged orbits are therefore
disjoint.

entries.statement: ∀ {R : Type u_1} (m : ℕ) (θ η : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace R m), Disjoint (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.arrowTaggedOrbit m CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.right θ) (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.arrowTaggedOrbit m CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Arrow.left η)
entries.source:
/-- The admissible-swap orbits tagged as forward and reverse are disjoint, regardless of their parameter values. -/
lemma arrowTaggedOrbit_right_left_disjoint {R : Type*} (m : ℕ) (θ η : ParamSpace R m) :
    Disjoint (arrowTaggedOrbit m Arrow.right θ) (arrowTaggedOrbit m Arrow.left η) := by
  rw [Set.disjoint_left]
  intro p hpR hpL
  rcases hpR with ⟨π, rfl⟩
  rcases hpL with ⟨σ, hσ⟩
  have htag := congrArg Prod.fst hσ
  simp [admissibleSourceSwapTagged] at htag
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.arrowTaggedOrbit_right_left_disjoint
entries.doc: The admissible-swap orbits tagged as forward and reverse are disjoint, regardless of their parameter values.

entries.statement: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.EffectiveRationalCADJob → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- The largest total degree charged by one supplied CAD input.  Generated CAD
projection polynomials are outputs of the cited run and are deliberately not
reclassified as inputs in this envelope. -/
def atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum (job : EffectiveRationalCADJob) : ℕ :=
  max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum job.input)
    (max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum job.secondInput)
      job.saturating.totalDegree)

entries.doc:

entries.statement: ∀ (m : ℕ) (execution : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution), execution.cadJob.DegreeBoundedBy (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasChargedDegreeBound m execution)
entries.source:
/-- The same charged maximum bounds the exact CAD input.  The generated CAD
projection family is intentionally absent: it is an output, not an input to the
universal complexity call. -/
theorem atlasCADJob_degreeBoundedBy_charged (m : ℕ)
    (execution : AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution) :
    execution.cadJob.DegreeBoundedBy (atlasChargedDegreeBound m execution) := by
  intro P hP
  exact (atlasCADJob_degreeBoundedBy_maximum execution.cadJob P hP).trans (by
    unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
    omega)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasCADJob_degreeBoundedBy_charged
entries.doc:
The same charged maximum bounds the exact CAD input.  The generated CAD
projection family is intentionally absent: it is an output, not an input to the
universal complexity call.

entries.statement: ∀ (job : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.EffectiveRationalCADJob), job.DegreeBoundedBy (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum job)
entries.source:
/-- Proves the stated mathematical property of atlas CADJob degree Bounded By maximum. -/
theorem atlasCADJob_degreeBoundedBy_maximum (job : EffectiveRationalCADJob) :
    job.DegreeBoundedBy (atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum job) := by
  intro P hP
  simp only [Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_singleton] at hP
  rcases hP with (hP | hP) | rfl
  · exact (Finset.le_sup hP).trans (by
      unfold atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
      omega)
  · exact (Finset.le_sup hP).trans (by
      unfold atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
      omega)
  · unfold atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
    omega
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasCADJob_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
entries.doc: Proves the stated mathematical property of atlas CADJob degree Bounded By maximum.

entries.statement: ℕ → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- Post-elimination degree envelope for the actual paper execution.  In
addition to the exact source-incidence degree, this finite maximum charges the
two supplied elimination jobs, their returned saturated elimination bases,
the dependent intersection job and its returned intersection basis, and the
CAD input that consumes that basis. -/
def atlasChargedDegreeBound (m : ℕ) (execution : AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution) : ℕ :=
  max (atlasSourceDegreeBound m)
    (max (atlasGroebnerJobDegreeMaximum execution.forwardEliminationJob)
      (max (atlasGroebnerJobDegreeMaximum execution.reverseEliminationJob)
        (max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
            execution.dependentPipeline.forwardResult.result.saturatedEliminationBasis)
          (max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
              execution.dependentPipeline.reverseResult.result.saturatedEliminationBasis)
            (max (atlasGroebnerJobDegreeMaximum
                execution.dependentPipeline.intersectionJob)
              (max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
                  execution.dependentPipeline.intersectionResult.result.intersectionBasis)
                (atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum execution.cadJob)))))))
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasChargedDegreeBound
entries.doc:
Post-elimination degree envelope for the actual paper execution.  In
addition to the exact source-incidence degree, this finite maximum charges the
two supplied elimination jobs, their returned saturated elimination bases,
the dependent intersection job and its returned intersection basis, and the
CAD input that consumes that basis.

entries.statement: ∀ (m : ℕ) (execution : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution), 1 ≤ CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasChargedDegreeBound m execution
entries.source:
/-- Proves the stated mathematical property of atlas Charged Degree Bound positive. -/
theorem atlasChargedDegreeBound_positive (m : ℕ)
    (execution : AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution) :
    1 ≤ atlasChargedDegreeBound m execution :=
  (atlasSourceDegreeBound_positive m).trans
    (atlasSourceDegreeBound_le_charged m execution)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasChargedDegreeBound_positive
entries.doc: Proves the stated mathematical property of atlas Charged Degree Bound positive.

entries.source:
/-- Evaluate the complex generic two-arrow incidence at `(t, theta, eta, s)`. -/
def atlasComplexIncidenceEval {m : ℕ} (t : CumVec ℂ)
    (theta eta : ParamSpace ℂ m) (s : Direction → ℂ) :
    AtlasComplexIncidenceCoord m → ℂ
  | Sum.inl ra => t ra.1 ra.2
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inl x) => paramEval theta x
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inr (Sum.inl x)) => paramEval eta x
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inr (Sum.inr b)) => s b

entries.doc: Evaluate the complex generic two-arrow incidence at `(t, theta, eta, s)`.

entries.statement: ℕ → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.Direction → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℂ)
entries.source:
/-- Complex Zariski closure of the observable projection of the saturated
generic two-arrow incidence.  This is complex algebraic elimination only; it
does not describe projection over real atomic witnesses. -/
def atlasComplexObservableProjectionClosure (m : ℕ) (b : Direction) :
    Set (CumVec ℂ) :=
  zariskiClosure { t | t ∈ bandSupportedCumulants (2 * m + 2) ∧
    ∃ theta ∈ bandSupportedParams m (2 * m + 2),
    ∃ eta ∈ bandSupportedParams m (2 * m + 2),
      forwardCumulantMap m (2 * m + 2) theta = t ∧
      reverseCumulantMap m (2 * m + 2) eta = t ∧
      (if b = .forward then theta ∈ genericParameterLocus m (2 * m + 2)
        else eta ∈ genericParameterLocus m (2 * m + 2)) }
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasComplexObservableProjectionClosure
entries.doc:
Complex Zariski closure of the observable projection of the saturated
generic two-arrow incidence.  This is complex algebraic elimination only; it
does not describe projection over real atomic witnesses.

entries.statement: ℕ → Finset (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℂ) → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℂ)
entries.source:
/-- The common complex zero set of a finite observable polynomial family. -/
def atlasComplexObservableZeroSet
    (m : ℕ) (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℂ)) : Set (CumVec ℂ) :=
  { t | t ∈ bandSupportedCumulants (2 * m + 2) ∧
    ∀ P ∈ family, MvPolynomial.eval (fun ra => t ra.1 ra.2) P = 0 }
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasComplexObservableZeroSet
entries.doc: The common complex zero set of a finite observable polynomial family.

entries.statement: ∀ (m : ℕ) (execution : CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution), execution.dependentPipeline.DegreeBoundedBy (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasChargedDegreeBound m execution)
entries.source:
/-- The finite maximum really bounds every input and returned basis charged by
the dependent two-arrow elimination/intersection pipeline. -/
theorem atlasDependentPipeline_degreeBoundedBy_charged (m : ℕ)
    (execution : AtlasCitedEffectiveExecution) :
    execution.dependentPipeline.DegreeBoundedBy
      (atlasChargedDegreeBound m execution) := by
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · exact fun P hP =>
      (atlasGroebnerJob_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
        execution.forwardEliminationJob P hP).trans (by
          unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
          omega)
  · exact fun P hP =>
      (atlasGroebnerJob_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
        execution.reverseEliminationJob P hP).trans (by
          unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
          omega)
  · exact fun P hP =>
      (atlasPolynomialFamily_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
        execution.dependentPipeline.forwardResult.result.saturatedEliminationBasis
          P hP).trans (by
            unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
            omega)
  · exact fun P hP =>
      (atlasPolynomialFamily_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
        execution.dependentPipeline.reverseResult.result.saturatedEliminationBasis
          P hP).trans (by
            unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
            omega)
  · exact fun P hP =>
      (atlasGroebnerJob_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
        execution.dependentPipeline.intersectionJob P hP).trans (by
          unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
          omega)
  · exact fun P hP =>
      (atlasPolynomialFamily_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
        execution.dependentPipeline.intersectionResult.result.intersectionBasis
          P hP).trans (by
            unfold atlasChargedDegreeBound
            omega)
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasDependentPipeline_degreeBoundedBy_charged
entries.doc:
The finite maximum really bounds every input and returned basis charged by
the dependent two-arrow elimination/intersection pipeline.

entries.source:
/-- Provides the stated computational structure for this data type. -/
local instance atlasGaussianRationalIrreducible :
    Fact (∀ q : ℚ, q ^ 2 ≠ (-1 : ℚ) + 0 * q) := by
  constructor
  intro q hq
  have hq' : q ^ 2 = (-1 : ℚ) := by simpa using hq
  have hnonneg : 0 ≤ q ^ 2 := sq_nonneg q
  rw [hq'] at hnonneg
  exact (not_le_of_gt neg_one_lt_zero) hnonneg
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasGaussianRationalIrreducible
entries.doc: Provides the stated computational structure for this data type.

entries.statement: {m : ℕ} → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℝ → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.ParamSpace ℝ m → (Fin (m + 2) → Fin (m + 2) → ℝ) → (Fin (m + 2) → Fin (m + 2) → ℝ) → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Evaluation of a full incidence polynomial at `(t, λ, w, z)`. -/
def atlasIncidenceEval {m : ℕ} (t : CumVec ℝ) (lam : ParamSpace ℝ m)
    (w z : Fin (m + 2) → Fin (m + 2) → ℝ) : AtlasIncidenceCoord m → ℝ
  | Sum.inl ra => t ra.1 ra.2
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inl coord) => realParamEval lam coord
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inr (j, h, isWeight)) => if isWeight then w j h else z j h
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasIncidenceEval
entries.doc: Evaluation of a full incidence polynomial at `(t, λ, w, z)`.

entries.statement: {m : ℕ} → Finset (MvPolynomial (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ) → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasFiberAssignment m)
entries.source:
/-- Zariski closure of a real projection, retained only as a diagnostic notion
used by the counterexample module.  It is deliberately not the semantics of any
atlas construction-trace operation: complex Groebner elimination cannot compute
this real witness projection. -/
def atlasIncidenceProjectionClosure {m : ℕ}
    (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ)) :
    Set (AtlasFiberAssignment m) :=
  { a | ∀ Q : MvPolynomial (AtlasFiberCoord m) ℝ,
      (∀ x : AtlasIncidenceCoord m → ℝ,
        (∀ P ∈ family, MvPolynomial.eval x P = 0) →
        MvPolynomial.eval (atlasForgetWitnesses x) Q = 0) →
      MvPolynomial.eval a Q = 0 }
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasIncidenceProjectionClosure
entries.doc:
Zariski closure of a real projection, retained only as a diagnostic notion
used by the counterexample module.  It is deliberately not the semantics of any
atlas construction-trace operation: complex Groebner elimination cannot compute
this real witness projection.

entries.statement: {m : ℕ} → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.FiberDecisionVariableBlock
entries.source:
/-- The block occupied by a concrete coordinate of the full incidence system. -/
def atlasIncidenceVariableBlock {m : ℕ} :
    AtlasIncidenceCoord m → FiberDecisionVariableBlock
  | Sum.inl _ => .observable
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inl _) => .loadingAndCumulants
  | Sum.inr (Sum.inr _) => .atomicWitnesses
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasIncidenceVariableBlock
entries.doc: The block occupied by a concrete coordinate of the full incidence system.

entries.statement: ℕ → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- Observable-coordinate count `q_K`, for `K = 2m+2`. -/
def atlasObservableCoordinateCount (m : ℕ) : ℕ :=
  let K := 2 * m + 2
  K * (K + 3) / 2 - 2
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasObservableCoordinateCount
entries.doc: Observable-coordinate count `q_K`, for `K = 2m+2`.

entries.statement: {m : ℕ} → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℝ → CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- A polynomial has constant sign on a cell. -/
def atlasObservableEval {m : ℕ} (t : CumVec ℝ) : AtlasIncidenceCoord m → ℝ
  | Sum.inl ra => t ra.1 ra.2
  | Sum.inr _ => 0
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasObservableEval
entries.doc: A polynomial has constant sign on a cell.

entries.statement: (m : ℕ) → Finset (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℝ) → Finset (MvPolynomial (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ)
entries.source:
/-- View an observable polynomial family inside the full incidence coordinate
space.  This is how the dependent intersection basis is supplied to the final
simultaneous real CAD/QE job. -/
noncomputable def atlasObservableIncidenceFamily (m : ℕ)
    (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℝ)) :
    Finset (MvPolynomial (AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ) := by
  classical
  exact family.image fun P => P.rename Sum.inl
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasObservableIncidenceFamily
entries.doc:
View an observable polynomial family inside the full incidence coordinate
space.  This is how the dependent intersection basis is supplied to the final
simultaneous real CAD/QE job.

entries.statement: DecidableEq (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℝ)
entries.source:
/-- Equality on observable polynomials used by the finite sign-oracle program. -/
local instance atlasObservablePolynomialDecidableEq :
    DecidableEq (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℝ) := Classical.decEq _
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasObservablePolynomialDecidableEq
entries.doc: Equality on observable polynomials used by the finite sign-oracle program.

entries.statement: ℕ → Finset (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℝ) → Set (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.CumVec ℝ)
entries.source:
/-- The common real zero set of a finite observable polynomial family. -/
def atlasObservableZeroSet
    (m : ℕ) (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (ℕ × ℕ) ℝ)) : Set (CumVec ℝ) :=
  { t | t ∈ bandSupportedCumulants (2 * m + 2) ∧
    ∀ P ∈ family, MvPolynomial.eval (fun ra => t ra.1 ra.2) P = 0 }
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasObservableZeroSet
entries.doc: The common real zero set of a finite observable polynomial family.

entries.statement: ℕ → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- Structural-coordinate count `p_K = (m+2)K-1`. -/
def atlasParameterCoordinateCount (m : ℕ) : ℕ := (m + 2) * (2 * m + 2) - 1
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasParameterCoordinateCount
entries.doc: Structural-coordinate count `p_K = (m+2)K-1`.

entries.statement: (m : ℕ) → DecidableEq (MvPolynomial (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ)

    DecidableEq (MvPolynomial (AtlasIncidenceCoord m) ℝ) := Classical.decEq _

entries.statement: {K : Type} → [inst : Field K] → [DecidableEq K] → {r : ℕ} → Finset (MvPolynomial (Fin r) K) → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- The largest total degree in one finite polynomial family.  This is a
paper-side bookkeeping operation: it is evaluated only after the cited
elimination output has been returned, so it does not pretend that the
pre-elimination incidence bound also bounds a Gröbner basis. -/
def atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum {K : Type} [Field K] [DecidableEq K]
    {r : ℕ} (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (Fin r) K)) : ℕ :=
  family.sup MvPolynomial.totalDegree
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum
entries.doc:
The largest total degree in one finite polynomial family.  This is a
paper-side bookkeeping operation: it is evaluated only after the cited
elimination output has been returned, so it does not pretend that the
pre-elimination incidence bound also bounds a Gröbner basis.

entries.statement: ∀ {K : Type} [inst : Field K] [inst_1 : DecidableEq K] {r : ℕ} (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (Fin r) K)), CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.EffectivePolynomialFamilyDegreeBoundedBy family (CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum family)
entries.source:
/-- The largest total degree charged by one supplied CAD input.  Generated CAD
projection polynomials are outputs of the cited run and are deliberately not
reclassified as inputs in this envelope. -/
def atlasCADJobDegreeMaximum (job : EffectiveRationalCADJob) : ℕ :=
  max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum job.input)
    (max (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum job.secondInput)
      job.saturating.totalDegree)

theorem atlasPolynomialFamily_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
    {K : Type} [Field K] [DecidableEq K] {r : ℕ}
    (family : Finset (MvPolynomial (Fin r) K)) :
    EffectivePolynomialFamilyDegreeBoundedBy family
      (atlasPolynomialFamilyDegreeMaximum family) := by
  intro P hP
  exact Finset.le_sup hP
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasPolynomialFamily_degreeBoundedBy_maximum
entries.doc:
The largest total degree charged by one supplied CAD input.  Generated CAD
projection polynomials are outputs of the cited run and are deliberately not
reclassified as inputs in this envelope.

entries.statement: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.PolynomialSign → ℕ

payload. -/
def atlasPolynomialSignCode : PolynomialSign → ℕ
  | .negative => 0
  | .zero => 1
  | .positive => 2
entries.name: CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1.atlasPolynomialSignCode
entries.doc:
Encode the three-valued sign alphabet by natural numbers for the machine
